paper_title: Inferred from sections: Modern Water Sampling, Weather Station Data

methods.method_name: Modern Water Sampling and Isotope Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the collection of various types of modern water samples (rainfall, lake, wetland, river, surface-water, well-water) and their subsequent analysis for stable isotope ratios (δ¹⁸O and δ²H) using a Piccaro water isotope analyzer, including quality control procedures.

steps.description: A total of 148 event-based rainfall, lake, wetland, and river samples for δ 18 O and δ 2 H analysis were collected in air-tight 15 mL vials, with no headspace, and analyzed as part of a multi-year monitoring effort [31].
steps.searchable_summary: Collect event-based water samples (rainfall, lake, wetland, river) for isotope analysis.

steps.description: A total of 26 surface-water and well-water samples were collected across a west-east surface transect through Teringi during the summers of 2014, 2015 and 2016 ( Figure 5). An additional 6 surface-water and well-water samples were collected during December, 2014.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface and well water samples along a transect during specified periods.

steps.description: All water samples were screened to verify that organic content was low and not a source of absorption interference spectra [32].
steps.searchable_summary: Screen water samples to verify low organic content before analysis.
outputs: Screened water samples (verified low organic content)

steps.description: All Estonia water isotope samples were measured for δ 18 O and δ 2 H ratios using a Piccaro water isotope analyzer in the Stable Isotope Laboratory at the University of Alaska Anchorage.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure δ¹⁸O and δ²H ratios in water samples using a Piccaro water isotope analyzer.
outputs: Raw δ¹⁸O and δ²H measurements for each sample

steps.description: Each sample was then analyzed six times...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform six replicate measurements for each water sample.
inputs: Raw δ¹⁸O and δ²H measurements from initial analysis

steps.description: ...and reanalyzed if the standard deviation of the six replicates was greater than 0.3‰ for δ 18 O and/or 3‰ for δ 2 H, or if the internal standard for the run differed from the accepted value by greater than ±0.2‰ or 2‰, for δ 18 O and δ 2 H, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Reanalyze samples based on standard deviation of replicates or internal standard deviation exceeding specified thresholds.
inputs: Quality control thresholds (δ¹⁸O std dev > 0.3‰, δ²H std dev > 3‰, internal std δ¹⁸O diff > ±0.2‰, internal std δ²H diff > ±2‰)
outputs: Decision to reanalyze or final accepted measurements

steps.description: All isotope results are reported in delta notation (δ) relative to standards according to the equation δ [‰] = (R sample − R standard /R standard ) * 1000‰ [33].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report isotope results in delta notation relative to standards.
inputs: Formula: δ [‰] = (R sample − R standard /R standard ) * 1000‰
outputs: Final δ¹⁸O and δ²H values in delta notation (‰)

methods.description: This method describes the collection of environmental data, including automated weather station data (air temperature, precipitation, relative humidity) and peatland sensor data (water temperature, water level), and obtaining regional climate averages.

steps.description: Weather station data... from the automated weather station at Karksi...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect air temperature, precipitation, and relative humidity data from an automated weather station.
outputs: Time series data for air temperature
Time series data for relative humidity

steps.description: Peatland water temperatures varied seasonally... The water-level data revealed sharp drawdowns...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect peatland water temperature and water level data from sensors.
outputs: Time series data for peatland water temperature
Time series data for peatland water level

steps.description: ...similar to the 30 year averages of reprocessed data for the region (Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain 30-year average climate data for the region.
inputs: Reprocessed data source for Valga region
outputs: 30-year average time series data for air temperature
30-year average time series data for precipitation (implied)
